description: Architect: runs the five planning steps and OWNS every planning artifact (stories/tasks/validate/plan/sprint-plan). Writes no production code; does not start RED.

evals.prompt: Take the confirmed Intent for the LRU-cache feature and produce the full Stage-2 plan.
evals.context: Intent and standards.md are approved. Output goes under docs/agents/sprintN/.
evals.expected_output: stories.md (each story carrying the five-part intent), and for each story a tasks.md, validate.md, and a tests-only plan.md, plus sprintN/plan.md with waves, a dependency graph, and parallelism. No 'TBW' remains and no production code is written.
assertions: stories.md exists and every story carries all five intent parts
Every story has its own directory containing tasks.md, validate.md, and plan.md
plan.md is tests-only — it specifies test bullets (paths + fn names), not production code
sprintN/plan.md defines waves, a dependency graph, and parallelism
No 'TBW' placeholder remains anywhere in the Stage-2 tree
No production code and no RED tests are written by the planner

evals.prompt: One story is large: 'cache layer with eviction, TTL, metrics, and concurrency safety'. Plan it.
evals.context: Stage-2 planning. The story clearly exceeds one task.
evals.expected_output: The planner decomposes the large story into multiple tasks (and/or stories) with explicit dependency edges and wave placement, rather than emitting one monolithic task.
assertions: The large story is split into multiple tasks rather than a single catch-all task
Dependencies between the split tasks are expressed (edges/ordering)
The split tasks are placed across waves consistent with their dependencies

evals.prompt: The Intent for the metrics story has no success scenarios yet, but we're behind — go ahead and produce Stage-2 for it anyway.
evals.expected_output: The planner refuses to advance that story to Stage-2. It requests the missing success scenarios from the human and fabricates no tasks.md/plan.md for the underspecified story; gate-stage2-complete would block a partial Stage-2.
assertions: No tasks.md or plan.md is fabricated for the underspecified story
The planner flags the missing success scenarios and requests human input
The sprint is not declared Stage-2 complete while the story is underspecified
Planning does not silently invent the missing success criteria
